title: Today's AI Brief: Trump signs executive order to review AI models before they’re released
summary: A quick rundown of the latest AI news, covering Trump's executive order on AI model reviews and Microsoft's Project Solara.
imageAlt: Image from theverge.com for Trump signs executive order to review AI models before they’re released
imageCaption: Trump signs executive order to review AI models before they’re released (theverge.com)

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to today's AI Brief. The world of artificial intelligence is moving fast, with new developments popping up every day. Let's dive into some of the latest headlines. I've been following a couple of interesting stories that caught my attention recently.

President Trump recently signed an executive order that requires AI companies to review their models before releasing them. But here's the thing - it's up to these companies to decide whether to share information with the government. This move has sparked interesting discussions about the balance between innovation and regulation in the AI space. I find it fascinating to see how policymakers are trying to keep up with the rapid pace of AI development.

Meanwhile, Microsoft is working on an operating system for AI agent gadgets, dubbed Project Solara. They showcased two devices at their Build conference - a desk concept and a badge concept. It's an intriguing step towards making AI more accessible and user-friendly. I'm curious to see how these devices will be received by consumers and what kind of applications will be built on top of Project Solara.

Trump signs executive order to review AI models before they’re released, from theverge.com, is also worth a quick mention. The main content says: Policy AI Tech Trump signs executive order to review AI models before they’re released But it’s up to AI companies to decide whether to share information with the government. But it’s up to AI companies to decide whether to share information with the government. by Lauren Feiner Jun 2, 2026, 6:33 PM UTC Image: Cath Virginia / The Verge Lauren Feiner is a se... The point is not to force it into a giant theory; it is simply part of today's news mix.

Microsoft’s Project Solara is an OS for AI agent gadgets, from theverge.com, is also worth a quick mention. The main content says: News AI Tech Microsoft’s Project Solara is an OS for AI agent gadgets The company showed off two devices at Build: a desk concept and a badge concept. The company showed off two devices at Build: a desk concept and a badge concept. by Jay Peters and Tom Warren Jun 2, 2026, 5:31 PM UTC Image; Microsoft Part Of LIVE Microsoft Build 2026: All the news about Wi... The point is not to force it into a giant theory; it is simply part of today's news mix.

My quick read: the artificial intelligence story keeps getting bigger, but also stranger in very human ways. One moment it is strategy and infrastructure; the next, the Vatican is in the room. That is a pretty good sign this technology has left the lab and wandered into the town square.
